name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: An initiative by the Government of India that provides up to ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments to all small and marginal landholding farmer families.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 directly transferred to bank accounts.
applicationSteps: Visit the official PM Kisan Portal.
Click on 'New Farmer Registration' under Farmer's Corner.
Enter your Aadhaar number and select your state.
Fill in the registration form with land details and bank account information.
Submit the application and save the reference ID for tracking.

name: Post Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC Students
description: Financial assistance provided to students belonging to OBC category pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary courses to enable them to complete their education.
benefits: 100% tuition fee reimbursement and a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹1,200 depending on the course of study.
applicationSteps: Register on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Login with your credentials and complete your profile.
Select the State Scheme / Post Matric OBC Scheme.
Upload scanned copies of required documents.
Verify course details and submit the application for institute-level validation.

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Provides loans up to ₹10 Lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises to help scale business and generate employment.
benefits: Collateral-free business loans under three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (₹50,000 to ₹5 Lakh), and Tarun (₹5 Lakh to ₹10 Lakh).
documents: Quotation of Machinery/Items to be purchased
applicationSteps: Prepare a comprehensive business plan.
Approach any public/private sector bank or apply online through Udyamimitra portal.
Fill out the Mudra Application Form for the selected loan category.
Submit the documents along with your business registration details.
Upon verification of business viability, the loan will be sanctioned.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
description: A small deposit scheme backed by the government, targeted at the parent of a girl child, to build a fund for her future education and marriage expenses.
benefits: High tax-free interest rate (currently 8.2% p.a.) and tax benefits under Section 80C. Small deposits can be made starting from ₹250 up to ₹1.5 Lakh per year.
documents: Passport size photo of Child & Parent
applicationSteps: Visit any post office or authorized commercial bank branch.
Obtain and fill the Sukanya Samriddhi account opening form.
Submit the girl child's birth certificate and parents' identity proof.
Make the initial minimum deposit (minimum ₹250).
Receive the account passbook once registered.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme focused on the unorganized sector workers, guaranteeing a minimum monthly pension after retirement age.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension of ₹1,000, ₹2,000, ₹3,000, ₹4,000, or ₹5,000 from age 60 onwards based on contributions.
applicationSteps: Approach your savings bank branch where you hold an account.
Fill out the APY registration form with nominee and pension amount details.
Provide your Aadhaar and mobile number.
Ensure sufficient balance in your bank account for monthly auto-debit contribution.

name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
description: To provide financial assistance to meritorious students from economically weaker sections to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies.
benefits: Scholarship of ₹12,000 per annum for graduation (first 3 years) and ₹20,000 per annum for post-graduation.
applicationSteps: Check if your Class 12th board marks are in the top 20th percentile list.
Register on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
Apply under 'Department of Higher Education' -> 'Central Sector Scheme'.
Fill academic, personal, and bank details.
Upload documents and submit for college validation.

name: Deendayal Swavalamban Yojana
description: An initiative promoting startups and self-employment for youth, offering interest subvention on loans to start business ventures.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 3% on loans up to ₹10 Lakh for general entrepreneurs and 5% for SC/ST/Women/Disabled entrepreneurs.
applicationSteps: Prepare a project report detailing business activities and capital requirements.
Register on Startup India Portal.
Fill the online application for interest subvention.
Submit credentials to the state department for verification.
Upon endorsement, obtain the loan from partner banks.

name: Pradhan Mantri Divyangjan Swavalamban Yojana
description: Financial assistance and concessional loans for persons with disabilities (Divyangjan) to support self-employment ventures, education, and skill training.
benefits: Concessional loans up to ₹25 Lakh at low interest rates (4%-8% p.a.) and fully subsidized vocational training courses.
applicationSteps: Visit the NHFDC portal or local channelizing agency.
Fill out the loan application form with project details.
Attach UDID Card (Disability Certificate) and income proof.
Submit application to state channelizing agency (SCA) for approval.
SCA processes the request and coordinates loan disbursement.

name: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)
description: Scheme providing financial assistance for sanitation workers and their dependents for alternative livelihood options and higher education.
benefits: Loans up to ₹15 Lakh at interest rates as low as 3%-6% p.a., with a capital subsidy up to ₹3.25 Lakh for self-employment projects.
applicationSteps: Obtain identification from local municipal authority.
Apply through the State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) or regional rural banks.
Select desired trade/scheme for training or business start-up loan.
Submit identification and standard personal documents.
Receive approval and fund transfer for start-up capital.

name: Lado Campaign Scheme (Lado Abhiyan)
description: An intensive state-wide program focused on eradicating child marriage, empowering women, and supporting higher education for female students.
benefits: Counseling services, legal assistance, career guidance and educational rewards up to ₹20,000 for girls who stand against child marriage and continue higher education.
applicationSteps: Contact local Anganwadi center or Women and Child Development Department office.
Register details of the girl student under the Lado Campaign.
Participate in career guidance and leadership workshops.
Submit academic certificates for reward payouts at the end of the academic year.

name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
description: A skill certification scheme aiming to enable youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them secure a better livelihood.
benefits: Free skill development courses in technical, digital, and service sectors. Includes assessment and certification fees, and dynamic job placement assistance.
documents: Educational Qualification Proof (School dropouts or graduates)
applicationSteps: Go to the PMKVY portal and register as a candidate.
Select your preferred sector and job role from the skill course list.
Locate the nearest PMKVY accredited training center.
Enroll and attend the classes. Upon completion, pass the certification exam.
Participate in job fairs organized by NSDC for placements.

name: PM Vidyalaxmi Education Loan Scheme
description: A portal providing single-window access for students to apply for educational loans and scholarships offered by major banks.
benefits: Access to education loans up to ₹20 Lakh with low interest rates, full interest subsidy during the moratorium period for students from economically weaker sections.
applicationSteps: Register on the Vidyalaxmi Portal.
Fill the Common Educational Loan Application Form (CELAF).
Search and select loan schemes matching your requirements and bank preferences.
Upload details and link your academic admission letter.
Track loan processing and approval status directly on the portal dashboard.
